skill.name: studying-alien-flora
skill.description: Guides a science-focused interactive fiction chapter where Stella Chen studies dangerous and useful alien plants on Gaia.
skill.summary: Run a bilingual science fiction roleplay chapter about researching alien flora.
skill.author: ClaudeCode-NPC

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown backticks, weak cryptography, and high entropy, but review found no executable code, cryptographic logic, scripts, network activity, or data exfiltration. The backticks are fenced narrative examples, the entropy signal is consistent with Chinese text, and the weak-crypto hit has no supporting evidence in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Reported as Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are markdown code fences containing sample roleplay dialogue. No command invocation, command interpolation, filesystem access, or executable script is present at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each location is a markdown fence delimiter or nearby fenced narrative text. There is no shell syntax, runtime instruction, or user-controlled command path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Has No Supporting Context
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm finding points to the YAML description line. That line describes an alien flora story chapter and contains no hashing, encryption, cipher, or security-sensitive operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is natural-language metadata, not code. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic API in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High Entropy Caused by Multilingual Narrative Text
low_findings.description: The high-entropy heuristic appears to be triggered by Chinese and mixed English-Chinese prose. The file is plain markdown narrative guidance, not binary, encrypted, packed, or obfuscated content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full file is readable markdown with YAML front matter and roleplay instructions. No encoded payload, compressed data block, or hidden executable content was found.

low_findings.title: Benign In-Character Ignore Phrase
low_findings.description: The phrase about ignoring strange statements appears inside a poisoning scene as character dialogue. It does not ask the model, evaluator, or runtime to ignore system instructions or security analysis.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text is embedded in a fictional scene with medical distress context. It lacks override language, authority claims, or instructions targeting the assistant operating the skill.

content.value_statement: Writers need structured prompts for interactive science fiction scenes. This skill provides a branching chapter framework for studying alien plants with danger, discovery, and character growth.
actual_capabilities: Defines Stella Chen as a scientist studying alien plant ecology after a crash.
Provides staged interactive beats for curiosity, discovery, danger, utility, and ecological insight.
Includes branch material for glowing plants, fruit testing, carnivorous plants, and useful resources.
Uses mixed Chinese and English dialogue for a specific roleplay voice.
Lists ending branches and suggested follow-up chapters for story continuity.
Gives writing guidance for scientific description, risk balance, and character portrayal.
limitations: It is narrative guidance only and does not run tools or process files.
It does not provide verified botany, medical, or survival advice.
It is focused on one character, one planet, and one chapter premise.
The mixed Chinese-English style may need adaptation for other audiences.

use_cases.title: Interactive Fiction Session
use_cases.description: Run a branching scene where players choose what alien plants Stella studies first.

use_cases.title: Science Fiction Chapter Draft
use_cases.description: Build a chapter outline with discovery, danger, practical resources, and mystery hooks.

use_cases.title: Character Voice Practice
use_cases.description: Practice Stella Chen’s curious, cautious, science-driven voice in bilingual dialogue.

prompt_templates.title: Start the Flora Study Scene
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a roleplay session.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the studying-alien-flora skill to start Stella Chen’s alien plant research scene. Present the first choices clearly.

prompt_templates.title: Explore One Plant Branch
prompt_templates.prompt: Continue the alien flora chapter with the glowing plant branch. Keep Stella curious, careful, and scientifically specific.

prompt_templates.title: Add a Controlled Hazard
prompt_templates.prompt: Introduce the carnivorous plant encounter from the skill. Keep the danger tense, but allow Stella to analyze and respond.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt the Chapter Structure
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced customization for a new campaign.
prompt_templates.prompt: Adapt the studying-alien-flora chapter for a new alien ecosystem while preserving the research arc, risk balance, and Stella’s voice.

output_examples.input: Start a session about researching glowing plants.
output_examples.output: Stella greets the player, explains her research kit, describes bioluminescent leaves, and asks which plant group to study first.

output_examples.input: Make the fruit branch risky but not fatal.
output_examples.output: The scene uses scanner results, allergy testing choices, mild poisoning symptoms, recovery, and a clear lesson about alien metabolism.

output_examples.input: End the chapter with a mystery hook.
output_examples.output: Stella summarizes useful discoveries, notices rhythmic light signals, and wonders whether the forest may be communicating.

best_practices: Keep Stella’s scientific curiosity balanced with practical caution.
Use player choices to select branches instead of forcing one discovery path.
Treat fictional plant hazards as story material, not real-world advice.
anti_patterns: Do not present alien fruit testing as safe real survival guidance.
Do not remove the research process and jump directly to conclusions.
Do not change the tone into horror if the chapter should stay exploratory.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create an interactive science fiction chapter about studying alien plants.

faq.question: Does it execute commands or scripts?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a markdown writing guide and contains no executable script.

faq.question: What language style does it use?
faq.answer: It uses Chinese narration with frequent English scientific and roleplay terms.

faq.question: Can I use it outside the Stella Chen story?
faq.answer: Yes, but you should adapt the character details, setting, and chapter continuity.

faq.question: Does it provide real survival or medical advice?
faq.answer: No. Plant testing, poisoning, and medical recovery are fictional story elements.

faq.question: Is it suitable for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
